panels.description: Total number of Celery workers responding to heartbeat ping. Checked every 60 seconds from the monitoring worker.
panels.title: Active workers

panels.description: Total count of connectors currently in a repeated error state. Yellow at 1+, red at 5+.
panels.title: Connectors in error

panels.description: Total tasks waiting across all Celery queues right now.
panels.title: Total queue depth

panels.description: p95 indexing task duration across all sources in the last 5 minutes.
panels.title: p95 task duration

panels.description: Percentage of indexing tasks that failed in the last 5 minutes.
panels.title: Task error rate

panels.description: Per-worker heartbeat status. Green = responding to ping. Red = not responding. Workers removed after 10 consecutive missed pings.
panels.title: Worker heartbeats

panels.description: Rate of index attempt status transitions per minute, grouped by status.
panels.title: Index attempt transitions / min

panels.description: Distribution of index attempt transitions by status over the last hour.
panels.title: Attempt transitions by status (1h)

panels.description: Time since each connector last completed a successful index. Sorted by staleness.

targets.expr: topk(20, time() - max by (cc_pair_id, connector_name, source, tenant_id) (onyx_connector_last_success_timestamp_seconds{tenant_id=~"$tenant_id", source=~"$source", connector_name=~"$connector_name"}))

panels.title: Connector staleness — age since last success

panels.description: Connectors that have failed repeatedly and entered an error state.

targets.expr: max by (cc_pair_id, connector_name, source, tenant_id) (onyx_connector_in_error_state{tenant_id=~"$tenant_id", source=~"$source", connector_name=~"$connector_name"}) == 1

panels.title: Connectors in error state

panels.description: Total documents indexed and total failed index attempts per connector.
panels.title: Docs Indexed & Failed Attempts per Connector

panels.description: Rate of completed indexing tasks per minute stacked by source. Successes and failures are separate series.
panels.title: Indexing throughput — success + failures stacked

panels.description: p95 as solid line, p50 as dashed line per source.
panels.title: Task latency — p95 (solid) + p50 (dashed) per source

panels.description: Completed indexing tasks per minute broken down by individual connector and outcome.

targets.expr: sum by (source, cc_pair_id, outcome) (rate(onyx_indexing_task_completed_total{source=~"$source", tenant_id=~"$tenant_id", cc_pair_id=~"$cc_pair_id"}[5m])) * 60

panels.title: Throughput by connector

panels.description: 95th percentile indexing task duration per source type.
panels.title: p95 duration by source

panels.description: Distribution of task execution times.
panels.title: Task runtime heatmap

panels.description: Rate of task retries, revocations, and rejections per minute. Should be near zero.
panels.title: Error events — retries / revocations / rejections

panels.description: Number of tasks waiting in each Celery queue.
panels.title: Queue depth

panels.description: Rate of change of queue depth per minute.
panels.title: Queue growth rate (tasks/min)

panels.description: Age of the oldest waiting task in each non-empty queue.
panels.title: Oldest task age per queue

panels.description: Redis memory consumption over time. Peak shown as a dashed line.
panels.title: Redis memory — used vs peak

panels.description: Ratio of OS-allocated memory to Redis-used memory. >1.5 = significant fragmentation. <1.0 = Redis is swapping to disk (critical).
panels.title: Redis fragmentation ratio

panels.description: Number of active Redis client connections. A steadily rising count indicates a connection leak.
panels.title: Redis connected clients

panels.description: Health of each Prometheus scrape target. Green = UP. Red = DOWN.
panels.title: Scrape target health
